factors.summary: Regulatory changes pose risks including increased costs and operational constraints.

evidence.text: We are subject to licensing and regulation as a property freight broker and are licensed by the U.S. Department of Transportation (DOT) to arrange for the transportation of property by motor vehicle. The DOT prescribes qualifications for acting in this capacity, including certain surety bonding requirements. We are also subject to regulation by the Federal Maritime Commission as an ocean freight forwarder and an NVOCC, and we maintain separate bonds and licenses for each.

factors.summary: Environmental regulations are creating costs and operational constraints.

evidence.text: Our freight carriers are subject to increasingly stringent laws protecting the environment, including those relating to climate change, which could directly or indirectly have a material adverse effect on our business. Future and existing environmental regulatory requirements in the U.S. and abroad could adversely affect operations and increase operating expenses, which in turn could increase our purchased transportation costs.

factors.summary: Geopolitical factors pose risks including conflicts and trade tensions.

evidence.text: Our operations are subject to the influences of significant political, governmental, and similar changes and our ability to respond to them, including: changes in political conditions and in governmental policies; changes in and compliance with international and domestic laws and regulations; and wars, civil unrest, acts of terrorism, and other conflicts.

factors.summary: Foreign currency fluctuations and exchange controls are creating risks.

evidence.text: As we continue to expand our business internationally, we expose the company to increased risk of loss from foreign currency fluctuations and exchange controls, as well as longer accounts receivable payment cycles. Foreign currency fluctuations could result in currency translation exchange gains or losses or could affect the book value of our assets and liabilities.

factors.summary: Trade tariffs and restrictions are creating risks in international operations.

evidence.text: Our international operations are subject to various risks, including: changes in tariffs, trade restrictions, trade agreements, and taxation; difficulties in managing or overseeing foreign operations and agents; limitations on the repatriation of funds because of foreign exchange controls; different liability standards; and intellectual property laws of countries which do not protect our rights in our intellectual property, including, but not limited to, our proprietary information systems, to the same extent as the laws of the United States.

factors.summary: The company faces potential litigation risks from transportation operations.

evidence.text: We are subject to claims arising from our transportation operations. We use the services of thousands of transportation companies in connection with our transportation operations. From time to time, the drivers employed and engaged by the carriers we contract with are involved in accidents which may result in serious personal injuries. The resulting types and/or amounts of damages may be excluded by or exceed the amount of insurance coverage maintained by the contracted carrier.

factors.summary: Interest rate fluctuations could impact the company's financial results.

evidence.text: The transportation industry historically has experienced cyclical fluctuations in financial results due to economic recession, downturns in business cycles of our customers, interest rate fluctuations, and other economic factors beyond our control.

factors.summary: Overall demand is accelerating, particularly in transportation services.

evidence.text: There were capacity constraints in nearly all of our transportation services. Additionally, we experienced a decrease in the length of haul in our North American truckload business in 2014 compared to 2013, which contributed to increased net revenue margin in our truckload transportation business.

factors.summary: Order backlog or book-to-bill is stable as there is no specific mention of growth or decline.

evidence.text: Our business model. We are primarily a service company. We add value and expertise in the procurement and execution of transportation and logistics, including sourcing of produce products for our customers.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting toward higher-margin segments, particularly in transportation services.

evidence.text: Our Transportation net revenue margin increased to 15.8 percent in 2014 from 15.3 percent in 2013, largely driven by an increase in transportation rates charged to our customers, partially offset by higher transportation costs.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating.

evidence.text: Our consolidated total revenues increased 5.6 percent in 2014 compared to 2013. Total Transportation revenues increased 7.7 percent to $11.9 billion in 2014 from $11.1 billion in 2013.

factors.summary: The transportation and logistics end markets are growing fastest, particularly in North America.

evidence.text: We experienced record North American truckload productivity levels in 2014, as measured by volumes per person. In 2015, we expect to grow our headcount to support our future volume growth.

factors.summary: Operational efficiency is improving, particularly in North American truckload productivity.

evidence.text: We experienced record North American truckload productivity levels in 2014, as measured by volumes per person. In 2015, we expect to grow our headcount to support our future volume growth.

factors.summary: The supply chain is improving, with capacity constraints leading to higher pricing.

evidence.text: There were capacity constraints in nearly all of our transportation services. Additionally, we experienced a decrease in the length of haul in our North American truckload business in 2014 compared to 2013, which contributed to increased net revenue margin in our truckload transportation business.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is increasing, particularly in transportation services.

evidence.text: Total purchased transportation and related services increased 7.2 percent in 2014 to $10.0 billion from $9.4 billion in 2013. This increase was due to higher volumes in nearly all of our transportation modes and higher transportation costs.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are stable as there is no specific mention of excessive buildup or shortage.

evidence.text: Our business model. We are primarily a service company. We add value and expertise in the procurement and execution of transportation and logistics, including sourcing of produce products for our customers.

factors.summary: No significant quality or safety issues are mentioned.

evidence.text: Our business model. We are primarily a service company. We add value and expertise in the procurement and execution of transportation and logistics, including sourcing of produce products for our customers.

factors.summary: Management has maintained forward guidance, indicating confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: In 2015, we expect to grow our headcount to support our future volume growth. Our weighted average basic and diluted shares outstanding decreased 6.2 percent and 6.1 percent respectively in 2014 compared to 2013, primarily due to the 8.5 million shares repurchased as part of accelerated share ('ASR') repurchase program initiated in 2013.

factors.summary: Management is confident, as indicated by investments in talent and expansion activities.

evidence.text: We experienced record North American truckload productivity levels in 2014, as measured by volumes per person. In 2015, we expect to grow our headcount to support our future volume growth.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include capacity constraints in transportation services and potential challenges in managing costs.

evidence.text: There were capacity constraints in nearly all of our transportation services. Additionally, we experienced a decrease in the length of haul in our North American truckload business in 2014 compared to 2013, which contributed to increased net revenue margin in our truckload transportation business.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on expanding its global network and acquiring complementary businesses.

evidence.text: In January 2015, we completed our acquisition of Freightquote.com, Inc. (''Freightquote''), a privately held freight broker based in Kansas City, Missouri. Freightquote provides services throughout North America. The acquisition enhances and brings synergies to our LTL and truckload businesses, and expands our e-commerce capabilities.

factors.summary: The company is sensitive to changes in transportation costs, fuel prices, and market demand.

evidence.text: Our truckload transportation costs increased approximately 10 percent, excluding the estimated impacts of the change in fuel. Excluding the estimated impact of the change in fuel, on average, our truckload rates increased approximately 11 percent in 2014.

factors.summary: Key catalysts include successful integration of recent acquisitions and ongoing productivity improvements.

evidence.text: In January 2015, we completed our acquisition of Freightquote.com, Inc. (''Freightquote''), a privately held freight broker based in Kansas City, Missouri. Freightquote provides services throughout North America. The acquisition enhances and brings synergies to our LTL and truckload businesses, and expands our e-commerce capabilities.

factors.summary: The company is maintaining its market share, particularly in transportation services.

evidence.text: Our Transportation net revenue margin increased to 15.8 percent in 2014 from 15.3 percent in 2013, largely driven by an increase in transportation rates charged to our customers, partially offset by higher transportation costs.

factors.summary: Competitive dynamics are intensifying with new entrants and aggressive rivals, but the company is differentiating through its global network and service offerings.

evidence.text: Our office network is a competitive advantage. Building local customer and contract carrier relationships has been an important part of our success, and our worldwide network of offices supports our core strategy of serving customers locally, nationally, and globally.

factors.summary: The company's competitive advantages include its global network, customer relationships, and service capabilities.

evidence.text: Our office network is a competitive advantage. Building local customer and contract carrier relationships has been an important part of our success, and our worldwide network of offices supports our core strategy of serving customers locally, nationally, and globally.

factors.summary: Customer concentration is stable, with the top 100 customers representing a significant portion of revenues.

evidence.text: Our customer base is very diverse and unconcentrated. Our top 100 customers represented approximately 37 percent of our total revenues and approximately 26 percent of our net revenues. Our largest customer was approximately two percent of our total revenues and approximately one percent of our total net revenues.

factors.summary: The company is maintaining its capital expenditure at around $50 million to $55 million for 2015, indicating confidence in future growth. Additionally, capex is expected to increase to support future volume growth and integration of acquisitions.

evidence.text: We anticipate capital expenditures in 2015 to be approximately $50 million to $55 million.
evidence.subsection: DISCLOSURES ABOUT CONTRACTUAL OBLIGATIONS AND COMMERCIAL CONTINGENCIES

evidence.text: In 2015, we expect to grow our headcount to support our future volume growth. We completed our acquisition of Freightquote.com, Inc. (''Freightquote''), a privately held freight broker based in Kansas City, Missouri.

factors.summary: The company has increased its debt leverage with the amended credit facility, raising the amount available from $500 million to $900 million. However, there is no specific mention of changes in debt levels or interest costs in the overview section.

evidence.text: In December 2014, we amended the revolving credit facility to increase the amount available from $500 million to $900 million and to extend the expiration date from October 2017 to December 2019.

Our business model. We are primarily a service company. We add value and expertise in the procurement and execution of transportation and logistics, including sourcing of produce products for our customers.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation has shown improvement, supported by higher net revenues and effective cost management, with evidence of stable cash flow from operating activities.

evidence.text: Cash flow from operating activities. We generated $513.4 million, $347.8 million, and $460.3 million of cash flow from operations in 2014, 2013, and 2012.
Net income increased 8.1 percent to $449.7 million in 2014 from $415.9 million in 2013. Free cash flow was $454.6 million in 2014 compared to $390.3 million in 2013.

factors.summary: The company is committed to maintaining shareholder returns through consistent dividend payments and share repurchases. While the company has shown dedication to returning value to shareholders, there are no specific details provided about future dividend increases or the sustainability of the repurchase programs beyond the current period. Additionally, the decrease in shares outstanding due to repurchases in 2014 compared to 2013 indicates active management of shareholder returns, but the reliance on market conditions and cash position for future repurchases introduces some uncertainty. Overall, the company's approach to shareholder returns is positive but has elements of mixed commitment due to variable factors influencing dividend and repurchase activities.

evidence.text: "We used $215.0 million, $220.3 million, and $275.4 million to pay cash dividends in 2014, 2013, and 2012."
"The number of shares we repurchase, if any, during future periods will vary based on our cash position, potential uses of our cash, and market conditions."

"Our weighted average basic and diluted shares outstanding decreased 6.2 percent and 6.1 percent respectively in 2014 compared to 2013, primarily due to the 8.5 million shares repurchased as part of accelerated share ('ASR') repurchase program initiated in 2013."

factors.summary: Economic conditions present significant risks, particularly due to potential recessions and downturns, which could adversely impact the business and lead to cyclical fluctuations in the transportation industry.

evidence.text: Economic recessions could have a significant, adverse impact on our business. The transportation industry historically has experienced cyclical fluctuations in financial results due to economic recession, downturns in business cycles of our customers, interest rate fluctuations, and other economic factors beyond our control.

Economic recessions could have a significant, adverse impact on our business. The transportation industry historically has experienced cyclical fluctuations in financial results due to economic recession, downturns in business cycles of our customers, interest rate fluctuations, and other economic factors beyond our control.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing mixed volume trends, with a decrease in overall freight volumes expected due to economic downturns, while unit volumes in transportation services are growing.

evidence.text: A reduction in overall freight volumes in the marketplace reduces our opportunities for growth. A significant portion of our freight is transactional or 'spot' market opportunities. The transactional market may be more impacted than the freight market by overall economic conditions.
Truckload volumes increased approximately 3 percent in 2014. LTL net revenues increased 8.1 percent to $258.9 million in 2014 from $239.5 million in 2013.

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated active and successful merger and acquisition (M&A) activity, particularly highlighted by the completion of the Freightquote acquisition in early 2015. This acquisition has been integrated successfully and is expected to bring significant synergies and expand service offerings, aligning with the company's strategic goals.

evidence.text: On December 31, 2014, we funded $359.4 million of the purchase price for the acquisition of Freightquote, into escrow accounts pursuant to the purchase agreement and completion of the acquisition in January 2015.

In January 2015, we completed our acquisition of Freightquote.com, Inc. (''Freightquote''), a privately held freight broker based in Kansas City, Missouri. Freightquote provides services throughout North America. The acquisition enhances and brings synergies to our LTL and truckload businesses, and expands our e-commerce capabilities.

factors.summary: The company exhibits mixed pricing power performance. While there is evidence of successful price increases in transportation services, particularly driven by higher volumes and increased pricing to customers, there are also concerns about potential constraints on pricing power due to higher carrier prices, which could reduce net revenue margins.

evidence.text: Higher carrier prices may result in decreased net revenue margin. Carriers can be expected to charge higher prices if market conditions warrant, or to cover higher operating expenses. Our net revenues and income from operations may decrease if we are unable to increase our pricing to our customers.
This increase in Transportation revenues was driven by higher volumes in nearly all of our transportation modes and increased pricing to our customers.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, particularly in transportation services, and fuel costs are fluctuating, acting as a pass-through and affecting net revenue margins. The company is attempting to offset these rising costs through pricing adjustments.

evidence.text: Changing fuel costs and interruptions of fuel supplies may have an impact on our net revenue margins. In our truckload transportation business, which is the largest source of our net revenues, fluctuating fuel prices may result in decreased net revenue margin. While our different pricing arrangements with customers and contracted carriers make it very difficult to measure the precise impact, we believe that fuel costs essentially act as a pass-through cost to our truckload business.

This increase was due to higher volumes in nearly all of our transportation modes and higher transportation costs. Our truckload transportation costs increased approximately 10 percent, excluding the estimated impacts of the change in fuel.

factors.summary: Labor costs are a significant and increasing expense, driven by headcount growth and incentive plans. However, managing these costs is challenging due to rapid market changes, which make it difficult to adjust staffing levels appropriately.

evidence.text: Personnel expenses are our largest expense. In order to maintain high variability in our business model, it is necessary to adjust staffing levels to changing market demands. In periods of rapid change, it is more difficult to match our staffing levels to our business needs. We have other expenses that are fixed for a period of time, and we may not be able to adequately adjust them in a period of rapid change in market demand.

Personnel expenses increased to $939.0 million from $826.7 million in 2013. The increase in personnel expense was due primarily to an increase in expenses related to incentive plans that are designed to keep expenses variable with changes in net revenues and profitability, in addition to average headcount growth of 2.7 percent in 2014.

factors.summary: The margin trajectory shows mixed performance, with pressures from factors like fuel costs and carrier pricing impacting net revenue margins, while also indicating expansion in gross and operating margins due to higher net revenue margins and effective cost management.

evidence.text: Margins are under pressure due to various factors including fuel costs and carrier pricing.

Higher carrier prices may result in decreased net revenue margin. Changing fuel costs and interruptions of fuel supplies may have an impact on our net revenue margins. Our net revenue margin declines in times of fluctuating fuel prices.
Gross and operating margins are expanding, driven by higher net revenue margins and cost management.

Income from operations increased 9.6 percent to $748.4 million in 2014 from $682.7 million in 2013. Income from operations as a percentage of net revenues increased to 37.3 percent in 2014 from 37.2 percent in 2013.

factors.summary: The company is effectively managing its expenses, particularly in reducing operating expenses as a percentage of net revenues. However, there are challenges in adjusting expenses, especially personnel costs, to meet changing market demands, which could impact future financial performance.

evidence.text: As a percentage of net revenues, operating expenses decreased to 62.7 percent in 2014 from 62.8 percent in 2013.

We may not be able to appropriately adjust our expenses to changing market demands. Personnel expenses are our largest expense. In order to maintain high variability in our business model, it is necessary to adjust staffing levels to changing market demands. In periods of rapid change, it is more difficult to match our staffing levels to our business needs. We have other expenses that are fixed for a period of time, and we may not be able to adequately adjust them in a period of rapid change in market demand.
